ticket.title: Split board claim into open/claim/move verbs
handoff: Current 'board claim' is overloaded — it creates a ticket in any column via --status (defaults to 'handoff' for relays, 'in-progress' for tickets). The verb 'claim' semantically means 'take ownership of existing work', not 'create a new item'. Splitting into three verbs clarifies intent and aligns with Rule01/Rule03.

Design agreed with user: (1) 'open <kind>' creates in Open column, no assignee, refuses --assignee/--handoff payload. (2) 'claim <kind>' creates in Active, self-assigned (defaults assignee to current agent); 'claim <ID>' flips an existing Open ticket to Active. (3) 'move <ID> <column>' handles all other transitions and carries --handoff/--next/--verify/--gotcha/--assumes/--cleanup payload. Nothing is ever created directly in Handoff.
next_steps: P1: Add 'open' subcommand to Commands enum in rs-hack/src/main.rs. Wraps existing handle_claim logic with status=open forced, rejects --assignee and --handoff flags at arg-parse time.

P2: Refactor 'claim' — when given --kind: create in Active with assignee defaulting to current agent (env CLAUDE_AGENT or 'agent:claude'); when given a bare ID: flip that ticket's @hack:status from 'open' to 'in-progress' and set assignee. Reject if ticket is not currently in Open.

P3: Add 'move <ID> <column>' subcommand. Accepts the same payload flags as current 'claim' (--handoff/--next/--verify/--gotcha/--assumes/--cleanup). Enforces the transition matrix already in hack-board (open→active, active→handoff|review, etc.). Appends payload annotations to the existing ticket's block in source.

P4: Update slash commands — /handoff calls 'move <current-ID> handoff --handoff ...' on the agent's existing relay (matches Rule03 better than today's create-new-in-handoff). /refine calls 'open' for each child relay it generates.
P5: Update CLAUDE.md 'Never pick IDs yourself' section with the new three-verb surface and examples.
P6: Deprecate old 'claim --status <col>' — warn on stderr for one release, then remove the flag.
verify: cargo run -- board open --kind task --file /tmp/x.rs --title 'demo' prints a T-ID and the ticket appears with status(open)
cargo run -- board claim --kind task --file /tmp/x.rs --title 'demo' prints a T-ID and the ticket appears with status(in-progress) and assignee set
cargo run -- board move <existing-R> handoff --handoff 'x' --next 'y' flips status and appends payload lines
/handoff end-to-end still produces a valid handoff-column relay update (now via move, not claim)
gotchas: Current /handoff and /refine implementations both call 'claim --status handoff'. Don't remove --status from claim until P4 ships, or those slash commands will break mid-migration.
assumes: Assumes CLAUDE_AGENT env var (or similar) is available to default the assignee in 'claim'. If not, we'll need a config lookup or require --assignee explicitly — revisit in P2.
Assumes the transition matrix for 'move' is already implemented server-side for the drag-and-drop UI, so the CLI can reuse that validation logic rather than reimplementing it.

sourceLines: //! @hack:ticket(R001-T4, "Electrobun: evaluate as Mac-native alternative to Bun server")
//! @hack:phase(P3)
//! @hack:status(open)
//! @hack:relay(R002, "Multi-worktree sync: worktree-aware claim + CRDT .hack/ + rebase-ids")
//! @hack:status(handoff)
//! @hack:assignee(agent:claude)
//! @hack:phase(P1)

//! @hack:handoff("Design doc at architecture/multi-worktree-sync.md. P2 LANDED (see R002-T1 in hack-board/src/server.ts): per-relay shards at .hack/events/<id>.jsonl, 'scan' event type keyed on content hash, legacy-log migration on first serve. diffTicket / diffAndLog / snapshot / replaySnapshot removed from server.ts; scan_disappeared in status.rs now walks shards with legacy fallback. P1 SCOPE GENERALIZED — see updated @hack:next(P1): instead of just `git worktree list`, use a gitignored `.hack/worktrees.json` registry that can declare local clones, git-worktree subtrees, and SSH-remote peers; `git worktree list` becomes one auto-discovery source, not the whole story. Phases remaining: P1 (sibling registry + claim), P3 (per-todo files), P4 (scoped merge driver), P5 (rebase-ids).")

//! @hack:next("P1: Sibling workspace registry for `board claim` anti-collision. Add gitignored `.hack/worktrees.json` with `{siblings: [{kind: local|git|remote, path, host?}]}`. On claim: union IDs across self + every sibling (local = subprocess, git = auto-discovered from `git worktree list --porcelain` merged with the JSON, remote = `ssh <host> rs-hack board tickets -f json -p <path>`). Compound sub-ticket scans (R007-Tn) query siblings the same way. Warn-per-site-fail and proceed; race-resolution is P5's job. New subcommand surface: `rs-hack board worktrees add|list|remove`. Original @hack:next: 'Walk siblings via git worktree list --porcelain, union IDs, take shared lock at <git-common-dir>/hack-id.lock.' — that remains the implementation for the git-subtree kind; other kinds are new.")
//! @hack:next("P3: Per-todo files .hack/todos/<id>.md. Dir instead of single file. Update both parse_todos in status.rs and parseTodos in server.ts. One-shot migration on first serve/status.")

//! @hack:next("P4: Scoped merge driver for _todos.jsonl / _renamed.jsonl. Hidden `rs-hack board _merge-events %O %A %B` subcommand (concat + sort by t,id,type + dedupe). .gitattributes + `git config merge.hack-events.driver` installed by `board init`. Per-relay shards do not depend on it.")

//! @hack:next("P5: `rs-hack board rebase-ids`. Detect duplicate @hack:ticket/@hack:relay IDs after cross-machine merge. Pick winner by earliest `git log --follow --diff-filter=A` timestamp on the annotation line. Rename loser via existing AST machinery, plus scoped string-replacement inside @hack: citation strings. Shard-rename the ticket's events file (OLD.jsonl → NEW.jsonl via `git mv`). Append to _renamed.jsonl. Dry-run by default.")
//! @hack:verify("cargo test -p rs-hack-arch")
//! @hack:verify("cargo test -p rs-hack-mcp")
//! @hack:verify("Smoke: two local siblings registered in .hack/worktrees.json; `board claim --kind relay` in each produces non-colliding R-numbers.")
//! @hack:verify("Smoke: remote sibling declared via ssh; `board claim` logs a warning when the host is unreachable and allocates anyway.")
//! @hack:cleanup("Consider @hack: citation sigil (e.g. {R008}) for unambiguous cross-reference — deferred; not required for phase 1.")

//! @hack:handoff("P1 LANDED. New rs-hack/src/worktrees.rs holds the .hack/worktrees.json registry (Sibling::{Local,Git,Remote}), git worktree list --porcelain auto-discovery, and the sibling-ID union scan (subprocess for local/git, ssh for remote). New 'rs-hack board worktrees add|list|remove' subcommands manage the file. handle_claim now unions board.tickets ∪ union_sibling_ids before picking max(id)+1, so all three ID paths (compound -Tn, standalone, bare relay) are sibling-aware. Per-sibling failures emit a stderr warning and proceed (race resolution stays P5's job). .hack/worktrees.json added to .gitignore. Smoke verified: two local siblings allocate R003/R004 not R003/R003; unreachable ssh peer warns and still allocates.")
//! @hack:next("P3: Per-todo files .hack/todos/<id>.md. Dir instead of single file. Update both parse_todos in status.rs and parseTodos in server.ts. One-shot migration on first serve/status.")

//! @hack:next("P4: Scoped merge driver for _todos.jsonl / _renamed.jsonl. Hidden 'rs-hack board _merge-events %O %A %B' subcommand (concat + sort by t,id,type + dedupe). .gitattributes + 'git config merge.hack-events.driver' installed by 'board init'. Per-relay shards do not depend on it.")

//! @hack:next("P5: 'rs-hack board rebase-ids'. Detect duplicate @hack:ticket/@hack:relay IDs after cross-machine merge. Pick winner by earliest 'git log --follow --diff-filter=A' timestamp on the annotation line. Rename loser via existing AST machinery, plus scoped string-replacement inside @hack: citation strings. Shard-rename the ticket's events file (OLD.jsonl → NEW.jsonl via 'git mv'). Append to _renamed.jsonl. Dry-run by default.")

//! @hack:gotcha("The ssh-shell-quote in worktrees.rs is intentionally minimal — paths with shell metachars get single-quoted. If a remote path has unusual chars, double-check the produced ssh command.")
//! @hack:relay(R003, "Split board claim into open/claim/move verbs")
//! @hack:status(review)
//! @hack:assignee(agent:claude)

//! @hack:handoff("Current 'board claim' is overloaded — it creates a ticket in any column via --status (defaults to 'handoff' for relays, 'in-progress' for tickets). The verb 'claim' semantically means 'take ownership of existing work', not 'create a new item'. Splitting into three verbs clarifies intent and aligns with Rule01/Rule03.")

//! @hack:handoff("Design agreed with user: (1) 'open <kind>' creates in Open column, no assignee, refuses --assignee/--handoff payload. (2) 'claim <kind>' creates in Active, self-assigned (defaults assignee to current agent); 'claim <ID>' flips an existing Open ticket to Active. (3) 'move <ID> <column>' handles all other transitions and carries --handoff/--next/--verify/--gotcha/--assumes/--cleanup payload. Nothing is ever created directly in Handoff.")

//! @hack:next("P1: Add 'open' subcommand to Commands enum in rs-hack/src/main.rs. Wraps existing handle_claim logic with status=open forced, rejects --assignee and --handoff flags at arg-parse time.")

//! @hack:next("P2: Refactor 'claim' — when given --kind: create in Active with assignee defaulting to current agent (env CLAUDE_AGENT or 'agent:claude'); when given a bare ID: flip that ticket's @hack:status from 'open' to 'in-progress' and set assignee. Reject if ticket is not currently in Open.")

//! @hack:next("P3: Add 'move <ID> <column>' subcommand. Accepts the same payload flags as current 'claim' (--handoff/--next/--verify/--gotcha/--assumes/--cleanup). Enforces the transition matrix already in hack-board (open→active, active→handoff|review, etc.). Appends payload annotations to the existing ticket's block in source.")

//! @hack:next("P4: Update slash commands — /handoff calls 'move <current-ID> handoff --handoff ...' on the agent's existing relay (matches Rule03 better than today's create-new-in-handoff). /refine calls 'open' for each child relay it generates.")
//! @hack:next("P5: Update CLAUDE.md 'Never pick IDs yourself' section with the new three-verb surface and examples.")
//! @hack:next("P6: Deprecate old 'claim --status <col>' — warn on stderr for one release, then remove the flag.")
//! @hack:verify("cargo run -- board open --kind task --file /tmp/x.rs --title 'demo' prints a T-ID and the ticket appears with status(open)")
//! @hack:verify("cargo run -- board claim --kind task --file /tmp/x.rs --title 'demo' prints a T-ID and the ticket appears with status(in-progress) and assignee set")
//! @hack:verify("cargo run -- board move <existing-R> handoff --handoff 'x' --next 'y' flips status and appends payload lines")
//! @hack:verify("/handoff end-to-end still produces a valid handoff-column relay update (now via move, not claim)")
//! @hack:gotcha("Current /handoff and /refine implementations both call 'claim --status handoff'. Don't remove --status from claim until P4 ships, or those slash commands will break mid-migration.")
//! @hack:assumes("Assumes CLAUDE_AGENT env var (or similar) is available to default the assignee in 'claim'. If not, we'll need a config lookup or require --assignee explicitly — revisit in P2.")
//! @hack:assumes("Assumes the transition matrix for 'move' is already implemented server-side for the drag-and-drop UI, so the CLI can reuse that validation logic rather than reimplementing it.")
//! @hack:relay(R004, "Kind-aware compound sub-ticket IDs (R011-B1/F2/T3)")
//! @hack:status(review)
//! @hack:assignee(agent:claude)

//! @hack:handoff("Sub-ticket allocator in handle_claim (rs-hack/src/main.rs:4984-5015) always emits <parent>-T<n> and filters by that prefix — --kind is preserved only as @hack:kind(...), not in the ID letter. Request: single counter per relay, letter reflects kind (B/F/T). So opening a bug then a feature then a task under R011 yields R011-B1, R011-F2, R011-T3.")
//! @hack:next("P1: Widen the compound prefix filter to <parent>-[BFT] so the counter spans all kinds under one parent. Pick the letter from force_kind at format time.")
//! @hack:next("P2: Epic? Sub-tickets-of-sub-tickets aren't a thing today — compound IDs are one level deep. Keep that invariant; just swap the letter.")
//! @hack:next("P3: Board/scanner already parses `<parent>-T<n>` via a regex or strip_prefix in ticket.rs — audit call sites for the T-literal and widen to [BFT].")
//! @hack:next("P4: Tests: add cases in rs-hack-arch/tests/dogfood.rs covering mixed-kind allocation order under one parent, and a legacy-bare-T01-coexistence case.")
//! @hack:verify("cargo run -- board claim --kind bug --parent R011 prints R011-B<n>; --kind feature prints R011-F<n>; counters share one sequence")
//! @hack:verify("cargo test -p rs-hack-arch passes")

//! @hack:gotcha("ticket.rs parses compound IDs in several places (epic inference at line ~369, sub-ticket matching at ~337) — all of them currently hardcode -T. Miss one and the new B/F IDs become invisible to the board.")
//! @hack:assumes("Assumes no external tooling greps for literal -T<n> in source; if anything does (CI, docs, scripts), it'll need updating in lockstep.")

//! @hack:handoff("Implemented. Sub-ticket allocator in handle_claim now emits <parent>-<L><n> with L ∈ {B,F,T} from --kind, and the counter is unified across kinds per parent (rs-hack/src/main.rs:4992-5022). Widened hack-board/src/server.ts bareRelayOf regex to /^(R\\d+)-[BFT]\\d+$/. Added test_mixed_kind_compound_ids_all_infer_parent in ticket.rs — parent inference via split('-') is letter-agnostic so no changes needed elsewhere.")
//! @hack:verify("cargo test -p rs-hack-arch passes (26/26)")
//! @hack:verify("End-to-end smoke in temp workspace: --kind bug/feature/task/bug under one parent → R001-B1, R001-F2, R001-T3, R001-B4 (unified counter, kind letter)")
